nodes.task:
Create the data layer for gates/blockers — pure functions, no I/O dependencies on CLI or TUI. Project root: /Users/twaldin/flt.

Create src/gates.ts exporting these typed functions:
- `scanGates(runsDir?: string): GateRow[]` — walks `~/.flt/runs/*/`, reads each `.gate-pending` JSON if present, joins with `run.json` to fill workflow + age. Returns rows shape: `{ runId, workflow, kind, reason, ageMs, runDir, payload }` where `kind` is one of `'human_gate' | 'node-fail' | 'reconcile-fail' | 'node-candidate'` (read from payload.kind). `reason` is payload.reason ?? payload.message ?? ''.
- `scanBlockers(runsDir?: string): BlockerRow[]` — walks `~/.flt/runs/*/artifacts/blocker_report.json`, parses each. Returns `{ runId, workflow, reason, ageMs, runDir, report }` where `reason` is report.reason ?? report.title ?? ''.
- `cleanStaleGates(runsDir?: string): { unlinked: string[] }` — for each `.gate-pending`, if the sibling run.json shows status `'cancelled'` or `'completed'`, unlink the gate file. Return list of unlinked paths.

The `runsDir` parameter defaults to `path.join(os.homedir(), '.flt', 'runs')`; tests override it with a tmp dir. Use `fs.readdirSync`, `fs.readFileSync`, `fs.statSync` (for ageMs from mtime), `fs.unlinkSync`. Do NOT swallow errors silently in the production path beyond per-run try/catch — if a single run dir is malformed, skip it but continue.

Reuse the existing `WorkflowRun` type from src/workflow/types.ts when reading run.json — do NOT redefine. If you need a narrower type for just `{ status, workflow }` reads, accept `Pick<WorkflowRun, 'status' | 'workflow'>`. NO `as any` or `as unknown as` casts (CLAUDE.md rule).

TDD: Write tests/unit/gates.test.ts FIRST. Watch them fail. Cases:
1. scanGates returns empty for empty runsDir.
2. scanGates surfaces a human_gate row given a fixture run-dir with run.json + .gate-pending containing `{kind:'human_gate', reason:'approve PR'}`.
3. scanGates surfaces node-fail, reconcile-fail, node-candidate rows correctly mapped.
4. scanGates skips runs whose run.json status is `'cancelled'` (don't show stale gates).
5. scanBlockers returns rows for runs with artifacts/blocker_report.json; empty for runs without.
6. cleanStaleGates unlinks .gate-pending in cancelled/completed runs and leaves running runs alone; returns list of unlinked paths.
7. ageMs is non-negative and roughly matches mtime delta.

Use Bun's test runner (`import { test, expect, describe, beforeEach, afterEach } from 'bun:test'`). Build fixtures under `Bun.tempDir()` or `fs.mkdtempSync`.

Files to create: src/gates.ts, tests/unit/gates.test.ts.
Files to modify: NONE.

Run `bun test` (full suite, not just gates.test.ts) and `bun run typecheck` (or `bunx tsc --noEmit` if no script). Both must pass.

flt workflow pass when tests are green and your coder.md handoff is written.

Fix the latent cleanup bug: `cancelWorkflow` in src/workflow/engine.ts:525 finalizes a run without unlinking `.gate-pending`, leaving stale gates that pollute the new `flt gates` view. Project root: /Users/twaldin/flt.

The pattern is already established in this file — see engine.ts:1134, 1152, 1180 (`try { unlinkSync(join(run.runDir, '.gate-pending')) } catch {}`). Add the same idempotent unlink to `cancelWorkflow` BEFORE the `finalizeRun(run)` call (after status is set to 'cancelled', before persistence). Guard with `if (run.runDir)`.

DO NOT modify the public surface of `cancelWorkflow` (it's called by src/commands/workflow.ts:workflowCancel). DO NOT rewrite finalizeRun or cleanupWorkflowWorktrees. This is a 3-line surgical fix.

TDD: Write tests/unit/workflow-cancel-gate-cleanup.test.ts FIRST. Watch it fail. Cases:
1. Setting up a workflow run with a `.gate-pending` file, calling `cancelWorkflow(runId)`, then asserting `.gate-pending` no longer exists.
2. Calling `cancelWorkflow` on a run with no `.gate-pending` is a no-op (does not throw).

Use the existing test helpers from tests/unit/workflow-human-gate.test.ts or tests/unit/workflow-engine-plumbing.test.ts as a template for setting up a WorkflowRun fixture and overriding `~/.flt`. NO `as any` casts.

Files to create: tests/unit/workflow-cancel-gate-cleanup.test.ts.
Files to modify: src/workflow/engine.ts (only the cancelWorkflow function, ~3 lines added).

Run `bun test` (FULL suite — must not regress workflow-human-gate, workflow-gc, etc). Must pass.

flt workflow pass when tests are green and your coder.md handoff is written.

Add `flt gates` and `flt blockers` top-level CLI commands, mirroring `flt list` / `flt activity`. Project root: /Users/twaldin/flt.

Depends on src/gates.ts from the gates-module node (use `scanGates`, `scanBlockers`, `cleanStaleGates`).

Create src/commands/gates.ts exporting:
- `gates(opts: { json?: boolean; watch?: boolean }): Promise<void>` — calls `cleanStaleGates()` then `scanGates()`. If `--json`, prints JSON.stringify(rows, null, 2). Otherwise prints tab-separated rows: header `AGE\tRUN\tWORKFLOW\tKIND\tREASON` then each row. Format AGE as compact human-readable (e.g. `2m`, `1h`, `3d`) using a small inline helper. Truncate REASON to 80 chars with ellipsis. If `--watch`, set up `fs.watch(runsDir, { recursive: true })` debounced ~200ms, re-render on each batch. SIGINT cleanly closes the watcher and exits 0.
- `blockers(opts: { json?: boolean; watch?: boolean }): Promise<void>` — same shape, calls `scanBlockers()`. No cleanStaleGates here.

Register both in src/cli.ts after the existing `activity` command registration. Keep options minimal: `--json`, `--watch`. Wrap action handler in try/catch matching the existing pattern in cli.ts (console.error + process.exit(1)).

DO NOT add new dependencies. Use `node:fs`, `node:path`, `node:os` only.

TDD: Write tests/integration/gates-cli.test.ts FIRST. Watch it fail. Cases:
1. `flt gates` against a fixture runsDir prints the expected tab-separated rows in stable order (sort by ageMs desc).
2. `flt gates --json` produces parseable JSON whose first row matches the fixture.
3. `flt blockers` against a fixture runsDir surfaces blocker_report.json rows.
4. `flt gates` prints only header (no rows) when runsDir has no pending gates.

Invoke the CLI by spawning `bun run src/cli.ts gates` (or however adjacent integration tests do it — check tests/integration/dag-execute.test.ts for the pattern). Override `~/.flt/runs` via env var or by passing runsDir; if neither path is wired, add a hidden `--runs-dir <path>` option used only by tests (document it as test-only in a one-line comment). Do NOT mock fs.

DO NOT implement --watch tests (timing-flaky); just smoke that the flag is accepted without crashing during arg parse.

NO `as any` casts.

Files to create: src/commands/gates.ts, tests/integration/gates-cli.test.ts.
Files to modify: src/cli.ts (register two commands).

Run `bun test` (FULL suite) and `bunx tsc --noEmit`. Both must pass.

flt workflow pass when tests are green and your coder.md handoff is written.

Add a TUI gates modal bound to `g` in normal mode. Mirror the shape of src/tui/modal-workflows.ts (278 lines — open/keypress/render + register in app.ts). Project root: /Users/twaldin/flt.

Depends on src/gates.ts from the gates-module node.

Create src/tui/modal-gates.ts exporting:
- `interface GatesModalState { rows: GateRow[]; selectedIndex: number; subPicker: { nodeId: string; candidates: string[]; index: number } | null; rejectPrompt: { reason: string } | null; cancelConfirm: boolean; blockerOverlay: BlockerRow | null; watcher: fs.FSWatcher | null }`
- `initialGatesModalState(): GatesModalState` — returns state with rows from `scanGates()` after `cleanStaleGates()`.
- `openGatesWatcher(state, onChange): void` — sets up debounced `fs.watch(~/.flt/runs, { recursive: true })` that re-runs scanGates and calls onChange.
- `closeGatesWatcher(state): void` — closes watcher, sets to null.
- `renderGatesModal(screen, state, layout): void` — 5-col layout (AGE, RUN, WORKFLOW, KIND, REASON) with kind-aware footer.
- `handleGatesKey(state, key, actions): boolean` — dispatches per kind: human_gate → a/x (x sets rejectPrompt), node-fail → r/s/a, reconcile-fail → r/a, node-candidate → Enter opens subPicker (j/k/Enter, label letters work too), blocker → v opens blockerOverlay, c sets cancelConfirm, d unlinks .gate-pending only.

Actions parameter is `{ approve, reject, nodeRetry, nodeSkip, nodeAbort, reconcileRetry, reconcileAbort, pickCandidate, cancelRun, dismissBlocker, refresh }` — each delegates to existing exported functions in src/commands/workflow.ts: `workflowApprove(runId, {candidate?, nodeId?})`, `workflowReject(runId, reason)`, `workflowNodeDecision('retry'|'skip'|'abort', runId, nodeId?)`, `workflowReconcileDecision('retry-reconcile'|'abort', runId)`, `workflowCancel(runId)`. CALL THESE — do not duplicate logic. For dismiss, just unlinkSync the .gate-pending file directly.

Wiring in src/tui:
- src/tui/types.ts — add `'gates'` to the Mode union (line 7) and add `gatesModal: GatesModalState | null` to AppState; initialize null in createInitialState.
- src/tui/keybinds.ts — add `'openGates'` to KeybindAction union, add `openGates: 'gates'` to the action→mode map, add `'openGates'` to the action lists, add `'gates'` to MODE_ORDER if it should be configurable, add default normal-mode binding `g: 'openGates'`.
- src/tui/app.ts — import { initialGatesModalState, openGatesWatcher, closeGatesWatcher, renderGatesModal, handleGatesKey } from './modal-gates'; mirror the workflows pattern (~lines 255, 1377-1430): add openGatesModal/closeGatesModal methods, add 'gates' case to mode dispatch + render, wire actions object for handleGatesKey to call src/commands/workflow.ts functions. After any action, refresh rows via scanGates and re-render.

fs.watch lifecycle MUST live in modal-gates.ts (open on modal open, close on modal close), NOT in app.ts — per bootstrap hard rule.

NO `as any` casts. NO new dependencies. NO test (no headless TUI test harness exists — verify manually with `bun run src/cli.ts tui` after making a fixture .gate-pending file in ~/.flt/runs/<some-run>/).

Files to create: src/tui/modal-gates.ts.
Files to modify: src/tui/types.ts, src/tui/keybinds.ts, src/tui/app.ts.

Run `bun test` (FULL suite) and `bunx tsc --noEmit`. Both must pass. Manual smoke: launch `bun run src/cli.ts tui`, press `g`, confirm modal opens (ok if rows are empty in your env).

flt workflow pass when tests are green and your coder.md handoff is written.
